description:
Consensus Medium (distributed + performance). Parallel mode shares ExUnit.Server / :cover / the code server with no per-module serialization: two workers on the same module race compile->load->restore, and two ExUnit.run/0 calls interleave one global server -> wrong classification + corrupted coverage. The byte-identity gate only runs against concurrency-safe FAKES, so it cannot catch real-ExUnit interference. Default of 1 is correct; the flag currently reads like a normal knob.

This is the immediate, cheap half of the CF2 fork (the real fix is CF2b module-partitioning). Honestly gate it now.

File: lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex:355-364 (documented), :628-633; mutation_loop.ex:181-214; flag plumbing in lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex.

## Fix
When the resolved max_concurrency > 1, emit a one-time stderr warning that parallel mode is EXPERIMENTAL and may produce incorrect kill/survive classification + corrupted coverage on real ExUnit/:cover backends, and that the safe path is the default (1). Keep the flag working; just stop presenting it as safe. Document the same in the --max-concurrency help text / README.

## Done
- max_concurrency > 1 prints the experimental warning once per run.
- Help text + README mark it experimental.
- A test asserts the warning fires for N>1 and is silent for N=1.

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 9, passed: 9 (mutation_pipeline v1-v9; 78 non-spike tests + 2 spike tests, 0 failures). Implementer correctly reported "stubs flipped: none" — all 9 were already execute: true.
- Requirements coverage:
  - mutagen.mutation_pipeline.r15 (max_concurrency path + "keep the flag working" + honest default-1): covered by test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_parallel_test.exs:354 ("warns once for max_concurrency > 1 and stays silent for max_concurrency: 1") plus the pre-existing serial/parallel equivalence + ordering tests in the same file. Would fail if: the warning were silent for N>1 (assert parallel_stderr =~ "--max-concurrency > 1 is EXPERIMENTAL" at :364 fails); fired more than once (warning_hits == 1 at :373 fails); leaked at N=1 (the `, ""}` match at :376 fails); or if gating broke the flag (the {:ok, %{results: ...}} destructures at :360/:376 fail, and run3 of the equivalence test serial==parallel would break).
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. mix.exs untouched — no new external deps. Warning uses plain IO.puts(:stderr, ...); no new runtime behavior beyond the one-shot stderr line.
- Project verification: PASS. mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean (exit 0). mix test green deterministically (641 tests, 0 failures at seeds 0/1/2/3; one intermittent flake reproduced ~1-in-5 random seeds is the known pre-existing cover_server race in async resolver tests — unrelated to this diff, which touches only the warning path/README/help). mix spec.check reports exactly errors=2 warnings=0, both pre-existing: mutagen.ast (stale grep defp alias_to_module|defp find_module_body) + mutagen.cli (archive_install_test telemetry.app load). No new findings — delta-gate held.
- Audit-expanded scope: none.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- Source-claim trace OK: run/1 -> run_with_beam_cache -> execute/2 -> execute_after_prime/2 calls warn_experimental_parallel_mode(resolve_max_concurrency(cfg)) on BOTH the serial and parallel branches (warn precedes the `if max_concurrency == 1` split at mutation_runner.ex:483,528). resolve_max_concurrency defaults nil->1, so untouched flag stays silent. Warn is a single IO.puts, once per run.
- The experimental warning is emitted via direct IO.puts(:stderr, ...) at mutation_runner.ex:637 and does NOT route through the cfg :capture_io seam (which only captures per-site test stderr at :758/:1411). The test's run_and_capture_stderr/1 wraps the whole run in the REAL ExUnit.CaptureIO.with_io(:stderr,...) (test :388), so it genuinely captures the warning. The CaptureIoStub at test:75 (wired at :145) returns "" only for the internal per-site capture and does not mask the warning — no false-green.
- Help text (lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex synopsis + flag doc + Caps) and README (flag table + Parallel mode section) both mark --max-concurrency > 1 EXPERIMENTAL with the kill/survive + corrupted-coverage rationale. Done criteria all met.
